compiled_state.title: As a Java developer, my project is detected as java-maven or java-gradle by detect-stack.sh
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add java-maven (pom.xml at root) and java-gradle (build.gradle at root) as distinct stack values to detect-stack.sh. Document the equivalence contract (scripts that treat java-maven and java-gradle as equivalent vs. differently). Update CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md to include both values in the accepted values list.

**Why**: Java projects currently fall through to 'unknown' stack detection, receiving no test/lint/format gates and no routing. This story establishes the detection foundation that all subsequent Java stories depend on.

**Scope**:
- IN: detect-stack.sh: add java-maven detection (pom.xml at root → 'java-maven'), java-gradle detection (build.gradle at root → 'java-gradle'); java-maven takes priority when both pom.xml and build.gradle present; Java detection ordered below Python/Rust/Go/Node/Ruby (not above); CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md: add java-maven and java-gradle to the stack accepted values list; equivalence contract documented in a comment block or adjacent doc: scripts treating both as equivalent (gate-2b, gate-2d, project-detect) vs. differently (commands.* writes, wrapper detection)
- OUT: onboarding commands.* defaults (Story F2), gate extensions (Story F3), project-detect.sh (Story F4), stretch SCs (Stories F5, F6)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, detect-stack.sh returns 'java-maven' on a project with pom.xml at the repo root and no build.gradle
  ← Satisfies: "detect-stack.sh adds java-maven (pom.xml at root)"
- When this story is complete, detect-stack.sh returns 'java-gradle' on a project with build.gradle at the repo root and no pom.xml
  ← Satisfies: "detect-stack.sh adds java-gradle (build.gradle at root)"
- When this story is complete, detect-stack.sh returns 'java-maven' on a project with BOTH pom.xml and build.gradle (java-maven takes priority)
  ← Satisfies: "java-maven takes priority when both present"
- When this story is complete, detect-stack.sh returns the expected non-Java stack for Python/Ruby/Node projects (regression test — Java detection does not shadow existing stacks)
  ← Satisfies: "Java detection below Python/Rust/Go/Node/Ruby"
- When this story is complete, CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md lists both java-maven and java-gradle in the stack accepted values section
  ← Satisfies: "CONFIGURATION-REFERENCE.md stack accepted values list updated to include both"
- When this story is complete, the equivalence contract is documented: gate-2b, gate-2d, project-detect treat java-maven and java-gradle as equivalent; commands.* writes and wrapper detection treat them differently
  ← Satisfies: "scripts treating both as equivalent vs. differently documented via equivalence contract"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all detection cases (pom.xml only, build.gradle only, both present, Python/Ruby/Node regression)

## Considerations

- [Reliability] Root-only detection: only detect pom.xml/build.gradle at the repository root (not in subdirectories) — consistent with how Python/Ruby/Node detection works in detect-stack.sh
- [Maintainability] The equivalence contract must be machine-readable enough for future stories (F3, F4) to reference — consider a comment block in detect-stack.sh or a companion .md file in plugins/dso/docs

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
